title: Streaming agent interface
summary: A Next.js agent surface with streamed responses, tool results, authentication, persistence, and model routing.
bestFor: Public AI products, internal copilots, and fast product iteration.

title: Managed enterprise RAG
summary: A grounded retrieval path built around Vertex AI, managed data services, evaluation, and enterprise controls.
bestFor: Governed knowledge assistants and data-heavy enterprise workloads.

title: Multi-agent team with ADK
summary: Specialized agents coordinated through Google's Agent Development Kit with deployable samples and managed runtime options.
bestFor: Teams standardizing agent roles, tools, sessions, and evaluation on Google Cloud.

title: Persistent agent worker plane
summary: Long-running workers, queues, databases, and service-to-service networking behind a separately deployed web experience.
bestFor: Background research, ingestion, browser jobs, and workloads that should not live inside a request lifecycle.

title: Tool-using agent service
summary: Agents, handoffs, guardrails, sessions, human-in-the-loop controls, and tracing composed as a backend service.
bestFor: Python agent services that need explicit handoffs and inspectable traces.

title: MCP tool and context plane
summary: A governed boundary for exposing tools and context to agents without coupling every integration to one model provider.
bestFor: Shared enterprise tools, GitHub operations, and replaceable model runtimes.

title: Durable agent workflow
summary: Retries, timeouts, durable state, signals, and human approvals separated from model calls and web requests.
bestFor: Long-running business processes where silent failure is unacceptable.

title: Agent observability pipeline
summary: Vendor-neutral traces, metrics, and logs that connect frontend requests to model calls, tools, workers, and failures.
bestFor: Cross-cloud systems that need one evidence trail across Vercel, Railway, and GCP.
